title: AparHub Webhook Provider Spec
description: Machine-executable specification for a webhook provider's verification requirements. Every field drives engine behaviour — nothing is documentation-only.

spec_version.description: Schema version. Pin to exact version — breaking changes increment major.

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier. Used as catalog lookup key and ingest URL segment.

name.description: Human-readable provider name shown in UI.

region_tags.description: Geographic relevance tags. india = India-first providers.

status.description: official = AparHub-maintained and CI-verified. community = merged PR, CI-verified. deprecated = no longer recommended.

tier_required.description: Minimum AparHub tier required to use this provider. Asymmetric/JWT = scale. mTLS = enterprise.

docs_url.description: Canonical documentation URL for this provider's webhook verification.

ip_allowlist.description: Provider's published source CIDRs. Pre-filled in customer IP allowlist config. Optional — omit if provider does not publish IPs.

standard_webhooks.description: Set true if provider is Standard Webhooks compliant (webhook-id, webhook-timestamp, webhook-signature headers with id.timestamp.body construction). When true, verification block may omit signing_input and sig_value — they are fixed by the standard.

verification.description: Complete verification specification. Drives the engine's strategy chain.

registration_challenge.description: One-time verification challenge at source registration. Omit if provider does not require it.

primary.description: Primary verification strategy executed on every inbound request.

replay_prevention.description: Replay attack prevention. Omit if provider sends no timestamp or nonce.

registration.description: Fields the customer must provide at source setup. Drives the wizard UI — every field here becomes a form input.

events.description: Event types this provider can send. Pre-populates the event filter UI. Optional — omit for providers with dynamic event catalogs.

test_harness.description: MANDATORY. Used by CI to verify spec correctness before merge. No spec without a test harness.

idempotency.description: Provider-declared idempotency key. AparHub extracts this automatically for deduplication. Customer override in SourceOverrides takes precedence if set.

key_source.description: header = extract from HTTP request header. body_field = JSON path into the request body.

key_field.description: For key_source=header: HTTP header name (case-insensitive). For key_source=body_field: dot-notation JSON path into the parsed request body. Use [n] for array indexing. Examples: id | data.payment.entity.id | [0].eventId | payload.event.id

HmacStrategy.title: HMAC
HmacStrategy.description: Hash-based message authentication. Covers ~65% of providers.

algo.description: Hash algorithm. sha256 strongly preferred. sha1 legacy-only (Facebook, Intercom, Twilio).

encoding.description: Digest encoding of the computed HMAC before comparison.

sig_value.description: CEL expression → string. Extracts the claimed signature from the request. Engine compares this against the computed HMAC digest. Examples: header('X-Razorpay-Signature') | header('Stripe-Signature').extract('v1={s}').s | header('X-Slack-Signature').trimPrefix('v0=')

signing_input.description: CEL expression → bytes|string. Constructs the exact byte sequence that gets fed into HMAC. Omit only when standard_webhooks: true (construction is fixed as id.timestamp.body). Examples: raw_body | header('Stripe-Signature').extract('t={ts}').ts + '.' + raw_body | 'v0:' + header('X-Slack-Request-Timestamp') + ':' + raw_body

secret_field.description: Name of the customer_field that holds the HMAC key. Defaults to 'webhook_secret' if omitted.

secret_encoding.description: Encoding of the stored secret before use as HMAC key. Standard Webhooks uses base64 (strip the whsec_ prefix, then base64-decode).

SharedSecretStrategy.title: Shared Secret
SharedSecretStrategy.description: Static token comparison. No body signing — weaker than HMAC. Use for providers that send a bearer token or static header value.

token_value.description: CEL expression → string. Extracts the token from the request. Example: header('Authorization').trimPrefix('Bearer ') | header('X-Webhook-Token') | query('token')

comparison.description: Always use constant_time to prevent timing attacks. exact is provided for providers that use non-secret tokens.

AsymmetricStrategy.title: Asymmetric Key
AsymmetricStrategy.description: RSA or ECDSA signature verification. Provider signs with private key; AparHub verifies with public key. Requires Scale+ tier.

algo.description: Signature algorithm. RS256/ES256 most common. PayPal uses sha256WithRSAEncryption.

sig_value.description: CEL expression → string (base64). Extracts the claimed signature from the request.

signing_input.description: CEL expression → bytes. Constructs the bytes that were signed. For most asymmetric providers this is the raw body. PayPal uses a 4-field concatenation.

key_source.description: How the public key is obtained. jwks_url = fetch from a JWKS endpoint. cert_in_header = public cert URL sent with each request. static_cert = fixed cert in provider settings.

jwks_url.description: JWKS endpoint URL. Required when key_source = jwks_url.

jwks_fetch_method.description: HTTP method for JWKS fetch. Plaid uses POST.

cert_header.description: Header containing the public cert URL. Required when key_source = cert_url_in_header. PayPal: PAYPAL-CERT-URL.

key_id_field.description: JWT header field or custom header containing the key ID for JWKS lookup. Usually 'kid'.

cache_ttl_seconds.description: How long to cache the public key. On verification failure, force-refresh once before rejecting.

refresh_on_unknown_key_id.description: Force refresh the JWKS cache if the key ID is not found. Handles key rotation.

jwt_location.description: Where the JWT lives in the request. header = custom header. payload_field = JWT is the body. authorization_bearer = Authorization: Bearer <jwt>.

jwt_header.description: Header name when jwt_location = header. Plaid: plaid-verification.

key_source.description: jwks_url = fetch public keys from JWKS endpoint. static_secret = HMAC JWT with a known secret. oauth2_discovery = fetch from .well-known/openid-configuration.

validate_claims.description: JWT claims to validate beyond signature.

iat_tolerance_seconds.description: Max age of issued-at claim. Doubles as replay prevention.

key_id_field.description: JWT header field (or custom header) identifying which JWK to use. Usually kid.

DatalessStrategy.title: Dataless Notification
DatalessStrategy.description: Provider sends minimal payload (event ID only). AparHub fetches the full event via API callback. Requires Scale+ tier.

event_id_path.description: JSON path to the event ID in the minimal payload. Example: id | value[0].subscriptionId

fetch_url_template.description: URL template for fetching full event. {event_id} is substituted. Example: https://api.paypal.com/v1/notifications/webhooks-events/{event_id}

auth_type.description: Auth method for the callback API request.

token_url.description: OAuth2 token endpoint. Required when auth_type = oauth2_client_credentials.

auth_field_client_id.description: customer_field name for OAuth2 client ID.

auth_field_client_secret.description: customer_field name for OAuth2 client secret.

MtlsStrategy.title: mTLS
MtlsStrategy.description: Mutual TLS — provider authenticates with a client certificate. Requires Enterprise tier. Handled at AparHub infrastructure level, not per-request.

ca_cert_url.description: URL to the provider's CA certificate for client cert validation.

fingerprint_sha256.description: Expected SHA-256 fingerprint of the provider's client certificate. Optional additional pin.

RegistrationChallenge.description: One-time verification challenge sent by the provider when a webhook URL is registered. AparHub handles automatically — customer never sees it.

method.description: HTTP method used by the provider to send the challenge.

challenge_param.description: Query parameter or body field containing the challenge value to echo back. Example: hub.challenge

mode_param.description: Query param that must equal a specific value for the challenge to be valid. Example: hub.mode (must equal 'subscribe').

mode_value.description: Expected value of mode_param. Example: subscribe

verify_token_param.description: Query param provider uses to echo back the verify token. Null for providers that use HMAC on the challenge instead (e.g. Zoom).

verify_token_field.description: customer_field name holding the verify token. AparHub validates it matches what the provider sends.

response_type.description: How to respond to the challenge. echo_challenge_value = return challenge param as plain text. json_challenge_value = return {challenge: <value>}. compute_hash = HMAC-based response (rare).

ReplayPrevention.description: Replay attack prevention layer. Applied after primary strategy succeeds.

mechanism.description: timestamp = check request timestamp is within tolerance window. nonce = check event ID has not been seen before.

timestamp_value.description: CEL expression → int (unix epoch seconds). Extracts the timestamp from the request. Example: int(header('X-Slack-Request-Timestamp')) | int(header('Stripe-Signature').extract('t={ts}').ts)

tolerance_seconds.description: Max age of the timestamp in seconds. Requests older than this are rejected. Default 5 minutes.

nonce_value.description: CEL expression → string. Extracts the nonce/event ID from the request for dedup store lookup. Required when mechanism = nonce.

nonce_ttl_seconds.description: How long to retain seen nonces. Should be >= tolerance_seconds.

CustomerField.description: A field the customer must provide at source registration. Each field becomes a form input in the setup wizard.

name.description: Internal key. Referenced in CEL expressions as params.<name> and in strategy secret_field.

type.description: secret = masked input, encrypted at rest. text = plain input. url = validated URL. select = dropdown.

help_text.description: Where to find this value in the provider dashboard. Be specific.

name.description: Event type string as sent by provider. Example: payment.captured

sample_payload.description: Example payload for this event type.

TestHarness.description: MANDATORY. CI runs the verification engine against this data and asserts the result matches expected_result. A spec without a passing test harness will not be merged.

test_secret.description: Known test secret. NOT a real production secret. Used only for CI signature computation.

sample_headers.description: HTTP headers exactly as received from the provider in a real request. Include all headers referenced by CEL expressions.

sample_payload.description: Raw body string exactly as received. Must be the exact bytes — no re-serialisation.

expected_result.description: What the verification engine should return for this input. Primary test case should be 'verified'. Add additional harness entries for failure cases.

additional_cases.description: Additional test cases for failure modes. Tampered payload, expired timestamp, wrong secret etc.

mutate.description: Mutations to apply to the base test case before running. Keys: headers, payload, secret.
